title: paraspalathus spicata
intro: Paraspalathus spicata, also known as the Spurred Vetch or Lathyrus, is a perennial flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. It is a legume that contains a toxin called ODAP (oxalyldiaminopropionic acid), which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. Paraspalathus spicata has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including rheumatism, fever, and digestive problems, and is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be found in herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, and capsules.
